slides.name: 8-Bit Orbit
slides.description: Pixel-art neon arcade aesthetic on a deep navy void.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a CRT screen at 2am: cyberpunk, gaming, web3, indie dev tools, hackathon demos. Just as good for a tech talk that wants to lean into nostalgic-digital craft, a synthwave brand deck, or a creative review that wants to feel like a console.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts where the dark neon palette would actively work against the message — quiet institutional finance disclosures, healthcare patient-facing materials, traditional luxury.

slides.name: Biennale Yellow
slides.description: Solar yellow on warm parchment with deep indigo serif and atmospheric sun-glow gradients.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like an art-biennale poster or a museum's annual programme: exhibition decks, arts-institution announcements, design conference brochures, curatorial pitches, literary publications, studio retrospectives. Equally good for any deck wanting Dutch-editorial atmosphere with an unmistakable single-color signature.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need visual punch or saturated multi-color energy — the warm-paper canvas and one-yellow palette are intentionally quiet and atmospheric.

slides.name: BlockFrame
slides.description: Neobrutalist deck with pastel-neon color blocks and chunky black borders.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel pop-graphic and design-led: indie SaaS launches, agency credentials, creative reviews, brand redesigns. Also a strong unexpected pick for tech, finance, or research when the speaker wants to land as confident and contemporary rather than buttoned-up.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that require quiet institutional restraint or traditional weight (regulated disclosures, formal legal briefs).

slides.name: Blue Professional
slides.description: Cream paper background with electric cobalt blue accents; clean modern professional.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel modern-considered and lightly authoritative: B2B SaaS pitches, consulting deliverables, advisory updates, investor reports. Also a clean, tasteful choice whenever you want to read as professional without going stiff — research synthesis, internal reviews, brand work for service businesses.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts where the deck should feel hot, playful, or intentionally informal — the cool electric-blue restraint will read as overly polished.

slides.name: Bold Poster
slides.description: Editorial poster aesthetic with massive Shrikhand display and a single fire-engine red accent.
slides.best_for: Anything that should land like a magazine cover: brand manifestos, founder vision decks, editorial / cultural pitches, creative reviews. Excellent any time you want a few words to feel like a poster — including unexpected fits like a tech keynote or a finance manifesto that wants to be quotable.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need to communicate dense information per slide — the layout is built around a few large statements, not paragraphs of detail.

slides.name: Broadside
slides.description: Dark editorial canvas with a single fire orange accent and bilingual Latin/Chinese type stack.
slides.best_for: Anything that should land like a broadside newspaper headline: brand manifestos, magazine and cultural pitches, design talks, bilingual EN/CN decks, founder vision statements. Also a striking pick for tech, research, or business decks that want a dramatic single-accent editorial feel.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need to feel quiet, warm, or institutionally traditional — the dark canvas with fire-orange accent commits to drama.

slides.name: Capsule
slides.description: Modular pill-shaped cards on warm bone with a full pastel-pop palette.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel modular, modern, and a little Y2K: lifestyle brands, creator portfolios, DTC launches, beauty / wellness, agency credentials. Also fun for a playful tech demo or a research deck that wants pop-art clarity instead of gravitas.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that require traditional institutional weight — the capsule shapes and pastel pops actively soften authority.

slides.name: Cartesian
slides.description: Quiet warm-neutral palette with classical Playfair serifs; tasteful and unhurried.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel quiet, considered, and grown-up: investment theses, white papers, advisory work, longform research, gallery / cultural decks. Also a strong choice for editorial features, founder reflections, or any deck where restraint is the message — including across tech and finance.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need visual heat, multiple accents, or a sense of urgency — the warm-neutral palette is intentionally low-energy.

slides.name: Cobalt Grid
slides.description: Electric cobalt serifs on a graph-paper canvas, anchored by stair-stepped pixel-glitch decorations and slim hairline rules.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a quietly serious design / research bulletin, art publication, or curated trend report. Strong for studio annuals, agency capabilities decks, design-research publications, architecture / art / academic decks, and any deck wanting one strict accent colour and a printed-ledger calmness rather than corporate polish.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need warmth, multi-colour energy, or a casual / playful voice — the strict cobalt + cream + grid palette is intentionally austere.

slides.name: Coral
slides.description: Cream and coral on near-black, set in oversized Bebas Neue.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel warm-graphic and editorial: fashion, beauty, fitness, F&B, lifestyle brands, agency credentials. Just as strong for a creator portfolio, a manifesto, or a tech / research deck that wants warmth and a single bold accent instead of corporate cool.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that should feel quiet or institutional — the coral accent and oversized Bebas Neue commit hard to a confident magazine voice.

slides.name: Creative Mode
slides.description: Cream paper canvas with confident multi-color (green, pink, orange, yellow) accents and Archivo Black display.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel design-led and confident: creative agency pitches, design studio decks, ad shop credentials, brand creative reviews, art-direction reviews. Also a great unexpected pick for a tech talk, research findings, or finance review when the speaker wants to lead with taste rather than convention.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that demand institutional restraint and a quiet authority — the saturated multi-accent palette will read as expressive, not formal.

slides.name: Daisy Days
slides.description: Cheerful pastel deck with hand-drawn daisies, stars, and rainbows. Friendly, soft, and warm.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel friendly, soft, and joyful: educational content, kids and family, wellness programs, community workshops, creator portfolios for craft / illustration. Also lovely for an unexpected playful internal kickoff, a wedding planning deck, or any moment where warmth is the message — including across tech or business contexts.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts where the audience explicitly expects authority and precision — the hand-drawn pastel SVG decorations are the opposite of buttoned-up.

slides.name: Editorial Forest
slides.description: Forest green, dusty pink, and warm cream meet Source Serif 4 in a quiet, intentional quarterly-review deck.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a considered editorial — quarterly reviews, internal readouts, studio updates, creative-agency presentations. Equally good for any deck that wants to feel warm and unhurried rather than corporate, including research recaps, book or program announcements, and team retrospectives.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need to feel urgent, punchy, or sales-driven — the palette and rhythm are intentionally quiet.

slides.name: Editorial Tri-Tone
slides.description: Three-color editorial system: dusty pink, mustard cream, and deep burgundy, set in Bricolage + Instrument Serif.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a fashion-magazine spread: editorial pitches, fashion brand decks, lifestyle media, art direction reviews. Equally good for any deck — including tech, research, or business — that wants tri-tone discipline and serif/sans contrast instead of the usual neutrals.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need to read as soft or comforting — the burgundy/pink/cream tri-tone is intentionally high-contrast and styled.

slides.name: Emerald Editorial
slides.description: A magazine-cover business deck: emerald + navy + paper, double-rule masthead ornaments, and a bold Bodoni-style display serif.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like the front of a serious magazine, including but not limited to leadership readouts, planning-office reviews, and strategy briefings. The double-rule masthead ornament gives it editorial gravitas without making it stiff — also a great unexpected pick for product launches or research recaps that want to feel considered rather than corporate.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need to read as quiet, neutral, or institutionally restrained — the emerald field is too saturated to disappear into the background.

slides.name: Grove
slides.description: Forest-green canvas with cream type, classical Playfair serifs, and a single rust accent.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel organic, considered, and grown-up: sustainability and wellness brands, outdoor / nature products, wineries and restaurants, literary or arts decks, advisory deliverables, bilingual EN/CN reports. Also a calm, distinctive choice for tech, research, or business decks that want patience over urgency.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need neon energy or rapid-fire pop — the forest-green canvas and Playfair serif commit to a slow, classical voice.

slides.name: Long Table
slides.description: Warm cream and rust-red supper-club aesthetic with bold uppercase grotesk headlines, Fraunces serifs, and pill-shaped outlined buttons.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a warm, intimate, modern hospitality / community brand: supper clubs, dinner series, small restaurants, creative-studio events, membership pitches, lifestyle and wine brands. Equally good for any deck wanting a single warm accent colour, mixed-weight typography, and a social-media-aware modern-editorial voice.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need corporate polish, technical density, or a cold / minimalist register — the rust-red palette and bold serif mix are intentionally warm and people-facing.

slides.name: Mat
slides.description: Dark sage canvas with bone paper and burnt-orange accent; mid-century modern with wood undertones.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel mid-century, tactile, and intentional: design studio credentials, architecture / interior brands, ceramics / craft / furniture, advisory decks. Also a warm, distinctive choice for tech, research, or business decks that want a considered analog feel instead of digital-cool.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need fast tech energy or institutional restraint — the muted sage and burnt-orange palette is intentionally warm and slow.

slides.name: Monochrome
slides.description: Ivory ledger paper with all-black type; Lora serif headlines, Jost body, no color at all.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a hand-typeset ledger: user research synthesis, white papers, longform reports, academic and policy briefs, advisory deliverables, bilingual EN/CN reports. Equally good for tech, design, or brand decks that want their words to be the only thing on the page.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need visual personality or color-led storytelling — the all-ink palette is intentionally austere.

slides.name: Neo-Grid Bold
slides.description: Editorial neo-brutalism with a single neon yellow accent on off-white paper.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel confident and editorial-graphic: design-led pitches, brand work, founder talks, conference keynotes. Excellent for stat-heavy slides, comparisons, and process flows. Just as strong for tech, research, or finance when the speaker wants to read as design-led rather than corporate.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need to feel quiet, traditional, or warm — the neon-yellow accent and uppercase display commit to a confident editorial voice.

slides.name: People's Platform (Block & Bold)
slides.description: Activist poster energy: blue, orange, red on cream, with Alfa Slab + Caveat Brush.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel honest, loud, and graphic: cultural commentary, manifestos, civic and community decks, design talks, campaign pitches. Excellent for founder-vision moments, mission statements, or any deck — including across industries — that wants protest-poster energy instead of corporate polish.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts where institutional restraint is the actual goal — the saturated political-poster palette commits hard to expressive energy.

slides.name: Pin & Paper
slides.description: Yellow paper with safety-pin illustrations, ink-blue handwritten Caveat, paper-grain texture.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel hand-crafted, warm, and literary: qualitative research findings, founder reflections, longform brand stories, workshop debriefs. The signature safety-pin illustrations and paper-grain texture make it especially good for any deck — including tech or business — that wants personality and warmth over polish.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need to feel digital-native polished or rigorously data-driven — handwritten Caveat is intentionally informal.

slides.name: Pink Script — After Hours
slides.description: Black canvas, hot pink accent, pearl-cream paper, Instrument Serif headlines: late-night editorial luxury.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel nocturnal, intentional, and a little luxe: fashion brand decks, creator personal brands, after-hours / nightlife / spirits launches, luxury product reveals, editorial features. Also a striking unexpected pick for a tech keynote, research synthesis, or business pitch that wants to land with magnetic confidence.
slides.avoid_for: Daytime corporate-professional and traditional B2B contexts where the dark canvas with hot-pink accent reads as too styled or too expressive.

slides.name: Playful
slides.description: Sun-warm peach background with Syne display: a friendly indie launch deck.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel warm, indie, and approachable: creator portfolios, indie product launches, lifestyle brands, small-business pitches, newsletter / community decks. Also welcoming for any deck — including tech or research — that wants to feel friendly and human rather than corporate.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts where institutional credibility matters more than warmth — the peach palette is intentionally informal.

slides.name: Raw Grid
slides.description: Neo-brutalist deck with thick borders, offset shadows, and a pink/sage/ink palette.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel direct and graphic-confident: founder pitches, accelerator demos, brand decks, indie launches, creator portfolios. Strong for stat slides, comparison tables, and process flows. Equally good for tech, research, or finance when the speaker wants the deck to feel scrappy-confident rather than buttoned-up.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need to feel soft, warm, or intentionally quiet — the brutalist borders and offset shadows commit to a graphic voice.

slides.name: Retro Windows
slides.description: Windows 95 chrome: gray title bars, MS Sans Serif, pixel typography, full nostalgia.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel knowingly nostalgic: retro gaming, Y2K-aesthetic brands, creator portfolios with a 90s vibe, tech-history talks, deliberately tongue-in-cheek decks. A great choice anywhere a playful retro reference is the entire point.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need to read as modern, elegant, or institutionally credible — the Win95 chrome will always read as a costume.

slides.name: Retro Zine
slides.description: Beige paper with green accent and Bebas Neue + Caveat: a riso-printed zine in HTML form.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel printed, lo-fi, and crafted: indie zines and publications, music / arts brands, creator portfolios, small-batch craft launches, community decks. Also a great underdog choice for tech, research, or business decks that want a riso-print warmth instead of digital polish.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that demand digital-native polish or fast modern-tech energy — the layered zine aesthetic intentionally feels handmade.

slides.name: Sakura Chroma
slides.description: Vintage Japanese cassette-package aesthetic: cream paper, diagonal rainbow ribbons, condensed bold type, JIS-style spec checkboxes.
occasion: indie hardware or analog studio brand
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a vintage Japanese cassette package or a TDK / Sony / Sakura Color product catalogue: indie hardware brand decks, music-label release schedules, analog studio retrospectives, zine and magazine pitches, kawaii-tech product launches, creative-studio annual reports. Equally good for any deck wanting bold colour, condensed display type, and a tactile printed-product personality.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need restrained, corporate, or quiet typography — the bold condensed lockups, ribbon stripes, and primary-colour palette are intentionally loud and product-page-y.

slides.name: Scatterbrain
slides.description: Post-it inspired: pastel sticky notes, Caveat handwriting, Shrikhand and Zilla Slab type stack.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel like a designer's whiteboard: brainstorms, workshops, creative-agency credentials, design-thinking sessions, ideation pitches, art-direction reviews. Equally fun for any deck — including tech, research, or business — that wants to read as in-progress thinking rather than polished conclusions.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that demand precision and institutional weight — the post-it sticky-note aesthetic intentionally reads as warm and unfinished.

slides.name: Signal
slides.description: Deep navy canvas with bone paper and a single muted-gold accent; institutional with quiet weight.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel weighty, considered, and credibly institutional: investor decks, board presentations, consulting deliverables, legal / policy briefs, advisory pitches. Also a strong choice for tech, research, or brand work that wants to read as quietly authoritative rather than loud.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that should feel hot, fast, or intentionally playful — the navy + gold restraint commits to a sober voice.

slides.name: Soft Editorial
slides.description: Cormorant Garamond serif on warm paper with sage, blush, and lemon accents.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel literary, elegant, and unhurried: editorial features, longform brand stories, gallery / museum decks, advisory deliverables, wedding / lifestyle media, founder essays. Equally good for tech, research, or business decks that want a Sunday-supplement warmth instead of corporate polish.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need visual heat or punch — the warm-paper palette and Cormorant serif are intentionally quiet.

slides.name: Stencil & Tablet
slides.description: Bone paper with stencil-cut headlines and a six-color earth palette: archaeology meets brand.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel archival, tactile, and weighty-graphic: museum and cultural-institution decks, art / architecture brands, longform research, heritage and craft brands, manifestos. A great choice anytime — including across tech and business — when you want the deck to feel like a field manual rather than a slide deck.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that demand digital-native polish or playful pop — the stencil-cut display and earth-tone palette commit to a deliberate analog feel.

slides.name: Studio
slides.description: Black canvas with electric-yellow type; high-voltage design studio aesthetic.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel electric and design-led: studio credentials, creative agency pitches, brand showcases, art-direction reviews, fashion / sneaker brand work. Also a striking unexpected choice for tech, research, or business decks where the speaker wants the deck to *be* a brand statement.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that should feel quiet or institutional — the black-and-electric-yellow palette is the loudest in the library.

slides.name: Vellum
slides.description: Deep navy canvas with warm-yellow Cormorant serifs and a single dusty teal accent. A quiet, scholarly aesthetic.
slides.best_for: Anything that should feel scholarly, literary, and quietly intelligent: research synthesis, white papers, academic and policy briefs, advisory deliverables, longform editorial pieces, founder reflections. Equally strong for any deck — including tech, business, or creator work — that wants a calm, considered atmosphere instead of energetic visuals.
slides.avoid_for: Contexts that need visual heat or pop — the navy + warm-yellow Cormorant aesthetic is intentionally low-tempo.

slides.name: Bold Signal
slides.description: Confident, bold, modern, high-impact — colored card on dark gradient with Archivo Black + Space Grotesk and large section numbers.
slides.best_for: High-impact presentations where a bold colored card and large section numbers create visual hierarchy. Ideal for startup pitches, product launches, and keynotes that need to command attention. The orange-on-dark gradient with Archivo Black display reads as confident and contemporary.
slides.avoid_for: Quiet, institutional, or traditional contexts where the bold colored cards and high-contrast dark gradient would feel too aggressive or design-forward.

slides.name: Electric Studio
slides.description: Bold, clean, professional, high contrast — split-panel layout with Manrope typography, accent blue (#4361ee), and quote-as-hero element.
slides.best_for: Professional presentations that need high contrast and clean structure. The two-panel vertical split with accent blue bars works well for agency pitches, corporate decks, and case studies where quote typography as a hero element adds gravitas.
slides.avoid_for: Decks that need warmth, playfulness, or organic aesthetics — the stark black/white split and bold blue accent are intentionally high-contrast and structured.

slides.name: Creative Voltage
slides.description: Bold, creative, energetic, retro-modern — electric blue and neon yellow split panels with Syne + Space Mono and halftone textures.
slides.best_for: Creative and energetic presentations that want to feel retro-modern: agency pitches, design conferences, entertainment brands, youth-oriented product launches. The electric blue (#0066ff) + neon yellow (#d4ff00) contrast with halftone textures makes it feel like a concert poster.
slides.avoid_for: Corporate, institutional, or traditional contexts where the neon palette and retro-modern energy would undermine credibility — this is intentionally loud and creative.

slides.name: Dark Botanical
slides.description: Elegant, sophisticated, artistic, premium — Cormorant serif with IBM Plex Sans, warm accents (pink, gold, terracotta) on deep dark background with abstract gradient shapes.
slides.best_for: Premium and artistic presentations: luxury brands, fashion decks, gallery exhibitions, editorial features. The elegant Cormorant serif with warm pink/gold/terracotta accents and abstract soft gradient circles creates a sophisticated, museum-quality aesthetic on a near-black canvas.
slides.avoid_for: Tech-heavy, data-dense, or casual contexts where the premium dark canvas and delicate serif typography would feel overdressed or hard to read at distance.

slides.name: Notebook Tabs
slides.description: Editorial, organized, elegant, tactile — cream paper card on dark background with colorful section tabs (mint, lavender, pink, sky, cream), Bodoni Moda serif, and binder hole decorations.
slides.best_for: Structured, multi-section presentations that benefit from a tactile notebook feel: research reports, educational content, workshop materials, consulting deliverables. The colorful section tabs and Bodoni Moda serif give it an editorial, organized elegance on a cream paper canvas.
slides.avoid_for: Quick pitch decks or high-energy presentations where the tactile, slow-reading notebook aesthetic works against the pace of delivery.

slides.name: Pastel Geometry
slides.description: Friendly, organized, modern, approachable — Plus Jakarta Sans on white card with pastel blue background and vertical pill decorations (pink, mint, sage, lavender, violet).
slides.best_for: Approachable, modern presentations that should feel organized and friendly: team presentations, onboarding decks, lifestyle brands, SaaS product tours, community workshops. The pastel pill decorations and rounded cards create a welcoming, non-intimidating visual language.
slides.avoid_for: High-stakes investor pitches or formal institutional contexts where the soft pastel palette reads as too casual or insufficiently authoritative.

slides.name: Split Pastel
slides.description: Playful, modern, friendly, creative — two-color vertical split (peach left, lavender right) with Outfit display, playful badge pills, and grid pattern overlay.
slides.best_for: Creative and friendly presentations: indie brand launches, creative portfolios, lifestyle products, community events. The peach (#f5e6dc) / lavender (#e4dff0) split with playful badge pills and grid overlay creates a warm, inviting, social-media-friendly aesthetic.
slides.avoid_for: Corporate, financial, or institutional contexts where the pastel split and playful badges would undermine the gravity of the content.

slides.name: Vintage Editorial
slides.description: Witty, confident, editorial, personality-driven — Fraunces serif (700/900) with Work Sans on cream background with abstract geometric accents (circle, line, dot).
slides.best_for: Personality-driven editorial presentations: brand storytelling, magazine pitches, creative essays, cultural commentary. The distinctive Fraunces serif, witty conversational style, and abstract geometric accents give it a confident, voice-forward identity on a warm cream canvas.
slides.avoid_for: Data-heavy or technical presentations where the editorial personality and cream canvas would distract from the substance of the content.

slides.name: Neon Cyber
slides.description: Futuristic, techy, confident — Clash Display + Satoshi on deep navy (#0a0f1c) with cyan (#00ffcc) and magenta (#ff00aa) accents, particle backgrounds and neon glow.
slides.best_for: Tech-forward presentations that want to feel like the future: product launches, developer conferences, gaming/esports, web3/crypto, AI and robotics pitches. The particle backgrounds, neon glow, and grid patterns on deep navy create an unmistakable cyberpunk atmosphere.
slides.avoid_for: Traditional, institutional, or non-tech audiences where the cyberpunk aesthetic and Fontshare fonts would feel alienating or overly niche.

slides.name: Terminal Green
slides.description: Developer-focused, hacker aesthetic — JetBrains Mono monospace on GitHub dark (#0d1117) with terminal green (#39d353), scan lines and blinking cursor.
slides.best_for: Developer and technical presentations where authenticity matters: open source talks, security conferences, hackathon demos, DevOps presentations. The JetBrains Mono monospace, scan lines, blinking cursor, and code syntax styling speak directly to a technical audience.
slides.avoid_for: Non-technical audiences, executive presentations, or any context where the monospace-only hacker aesthetic would feel exclusionary or hard to read.

slides.name: Swiss Modern
slides.description: Clean, precise, Bauhaus-inspired — Archivo (800) + Nunito on pure white with red accent (#ff3300), visible grid and asymmetric layouts.
slides.best_for: Design-conscious presentations that value precision and structure: design conferences, architecture presentations, research findings, strategy decks. The visible grid, asymmetric layouts, geometric shapes, and red accent on pure white create a timeless Swiss/Bauhaus clarity.
slides.avoid_for: Warm, playful, or casual contexts where the strict geometric precision and red-on-white palette would feel cold or overly intellectual.

slides.name: Paper & Ink
slides.description: Editorial, literary, thoughtful — Cormorant Garamond + Source Serif 4 on warm cream (#faf9f7) with charcoal (#1a1a1a) and crimson accent (#c41e3a), drop caps and pull quotes.
slides.best_for: Literary and editorial presentations where the written word is paramount: book launches, editorial features, academic lectures, longform research. The Cormorant Garamond serif, drop caps, pull quotes, and elegant horizontal rules on warm cream create a thoughtful, publication-quality aesthetic.
slides.avoid_for: Visual-heavy or data-dense presentations where the literary focus and serif-heavy typography would compete with charts, images, or complex data visualizations.
